Meta.goal: Replace env-resolved two-slot claude_config_dir with a single explicit field decoupled from AISW_ENV; fail-fast at startup when the dir is missing.

Decisions.Decision: Fail-fast lives in __main__._amain (runtime), NOT a Settings model_validator — Settings construction must stay filesystem-free so unit tests can build it with the default path that does not exist in CI. Source: pydantic-settings purity + existing test patterns (Settings(_env_file=None) used throughout tests/unit).

claude_config_dir is required for the bot regardless of stage0_backend, because the Stage-1 wiki runner and cron consumer always spawn the Claude CLI with it (__main__ guards at the wiki-runner and cron-consumer wiring were unconditional). So a single global startup check is correct; per-callsite checks are removed as dead.

Chunk.title: settings.py claude_config_dir single field + fail-fast + .env/tests/graph
Chunk.outputs: src/ai_steward_wiki/settings.py (single field, no property, INV-6 kept), src/ai_steward_wiki/__main__.py (_require_claude_config_dir + 3 dead guards removed), .env.example (AISW_CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR), docs/knowledge-graph.xml (Settings node), tests/unit/classifier/test_settings_inv6.py (updated), tests/unit/test_settings_config_dir.py (new: single-field + fail-fast)
Chunk.verification: make total-test exits 0 (ruff, ruff-format, mypy, grace lint, inv-lint, pytest unit + coverage>=80%)
